summary: FPV drones were used to kill two Russian soldiers on motorcycles assaulting Ukrainian positions and to eliminate a Russian soldier who attempted to feign surrender before fleeing toward his comrades. Ukrainian forces reportedly survived a Russian FPV drone attack in the Kostiantynivka direction, with the 36th Marine Brigade using drone detectors to anticipate the strike. Russian operators from the Rubicon Center claimed 105 targets destroyed on April 16, including Ukrainian personnel and fixed-wing UAVs, and 1 target destroyed on April 17, including personnel, fixed-wing UAVs, long-range UAVs, and logistics routes, while also cooperating with Russian Aerospace Forces to strike Ukrainian UAV launch points. Ukrainian electronic warfare reportedly interfered with FPV drones, issuing a 15-second landing command.
